summary: v6 WINS on 2/8 production text-heavy corpora (hexa_79k +0.97pp, audit_26k +0.08pp) + ties v4 on synthetic lr_80k. v6 LOSES to v1 on n6_atlas all sizes (4K/8K/16K/79K) by 0-2pp — n6 atlas repetition topology favors greedy LZ

analysis.verdict: v6 closes gap vs v2/v3-o2/v4 toward v1 but does NOT beat v1; lazy-match Storer-Szymanski advantage neutralized by n6 atlas repetition topology that is already greedy-optimal
v6 WINS by 1pp — JSONL stream where lazy 1-byte literal + longer match at i+1 beats greedy 3-byte match at i (within-32KB sub-optimality REAL on this corpus)
v6 wins marginally (21B / 26624B = 0.08pp); structured-audit class also benefits from lazy match
floor ceiling all variants tied — long-range repetition saturates encoder regardless of axis

K_canonical_rationale: Storer-Szymanski 1982 canonical form is K=1 (look one position ahead). Strict-greater rule (next_len > pend_len) captures net gain in expectation given literal cost ~9 bits and match-length-extension cost ~1-2 bits.
K_inf_full_optimal: DEFERRED to A26 sparse PPM-D ω-cycle
memory_K1: O(N) state per position (no DP table)
memory_K_inf_estimate: O(N × W) full DP table — 79KB × 32KB ≈ 2.5GB (F-A18-V6-4 200MB budget breach)

aggregate_delta_projection: +0.3 to +0.5pp on text-heavy + structured-audit class subset (corpus-class-conditional)
verdict_rationale: v6 lazy-match captures within-32KB sub-optimality on hexa-lang JSONL aot_cache and structured-audit but FALSIFIED on n6 atlas (production-class CEILING — n6 atlas greedy-already-optimal). Aggregate delta +0.3-0.5pp insufficient to bridge 1.70pp residual gap.
